questionHtml: Suggest two reasons why solid calcium has a greater density than solid potassium.
markschemeHtml:
Any two of:

stronger metallic bonding

smaller ionic/atomic radius

 

two electrons per atom are delocalized

OR

greater ionic charge

 

greater atomic mass

 

Do not accept just “heavier” or “more massive” without reference to atomic mass.

[2 marks]

meta.title: Suggest two reasons why solid calcium has a greater density than solid potassium.
